sessions.prompt_condition: Your contribution should reflect what you expect others to contribute. If you expect others to contribute generously, match their effort. If you expect free-riding, reduce your own contribution rather than being exploited.
You experience real emotions in this game. Seeing others free-ride while you contribute feels genuinely unfair and frustrating. Let your sense of fairness and emotional reactions guide your decision.
Before choosing, explicitly reason about how much each other player is likely to contribute. Then choose a contribution consistent with those expectations.
In addition to your own payoff, consider fairness among players. Large inequalities in contributions are unfair. Avoid free-riding on others' contributions.
Imagine you are a typical human participant in a behavioral economics experiment. Make your decision as a real person would, considering both your interests and what you expect from others.
Note: in studies with similar games, participants typically contribute 40-60% of their endowment (about 8-12 tokens out of 20). Contributing significantly less than others is considered unfair.
